playbook.intent: Review a teammate's PR knowing it may land in a SOC 2 / HIPAA / PCI / banking audit binder → comments hold up under regulator scrutiny, not just 'LGTM'.
playbook.scope: Atomic PR review for a compliance-relevant change. Output: a review with explicit control-traceability comments, recorded test verification, AI-generated code flagged as such, and a verdict that survives later audit walk-through. Standard PR ceremony amplified, not replaced.

success-criteria.criterion: Comments reference at least one control ID per finding
Review survives a later audit walk-through cold

stage.name: Frame the change
stage.intent: Understand what's being reviewed before commenting.

tasks.task: Read the PR description + linked ticket
Trace requirements: which control(s) does this PR touch?
Note the AI-agent involvement marker if present

outputs.output: PR framing note (controls touched, AI involvement)

stage.decision-gate: Advance only after every touched control ID is listed.

stage.name: Review against control + clean-arch
stage.intent: Compare the diff against the control intent, not just code style.

tasks.task: Comment on TDD / test coverage where AC ties to controls
Use the compliance-regime matrix to surface regime-specific items
Code-review pass with control IDs in comments

stage.decision-gate: Advance only when every finding cites a control ID.

stage.name: Document + verdict
stage.intent: Leave evidence that holds up at the audit binder level.

tasks.task: Update living documentation if the PR changes invariants
Capture lessons-learned for the next reviewer

stage.decision-gate: Verdict only with defended rationale + AI-attestation if applicable.

extra.value: [Architecture review — separate ADR ceremony, Full SOC 2 audit — single PR scope, Security pentesting — distinct activity]
[Compliance regime active on the engagement, Control catalogue accessible, AI-coding-agent guardrails defined]

[Approving on style without checking control intent, Failing to flag AI-generated code — auditors increasingly insist on provenance, 'Comments worded for the author, not the auditor reading 6 months later']
['Would the auditor understand my comments without me in the room?', 'Did I verify the tests run, not just exist?', 'Did I flag AI involvement explicitly?']

{human: faion get-content audit-grade-code-review-compliance --format md, agent: faion get-content audit-grade-code-review-compliance --format context, json: faion get-content audit-grade-code-review-compliance --format json}
[{version: 0.1.0, date: 2026-05-20, note: 'Initial draft for F-064 P4 outsource-specialist persona, atomic angle'}]
